full_text:
**📈**** Gutted Gulf oil could roar back in months if Hormuz opens - Goldman Sachs
**
👉 Crude output from the Gulf region slumped hard in April to 14.5 million barrels a day - a brutal 57% drop from pre-war levels. But Goldman Sachs estimates a rebound is possible within months if the Strait of Hormuz reopens fully and stays secure. 

Cautiously optimistic, it cites limited physical damage to oil fields and expectations that production can increase, helped by spare capacity in Saudi Arabia and the UAE.

**But here’s the problem:**

🔴 Logistics are tight, as empty tanker capacity in the Gulf has dropped by around 130 million barrels (~50%)

🔴 Prolonged shutdowns of wells can weaken flow rates — especially in low-pressure fields — meaning repairs and maintenance are required before full output returns

🔴 External estimates suggest Gulf producers could recover ~70% of lost output within three months and ~88% within six. 

📌 The longer Hormuz stays closed, the slower and messier the recovery becomes, notes the Wall Street giant.

Of course, [none of this damage](https://t.me/geopolitics_prime/68693) would have happened without the US war unleashed against Iran, its [blockade of the Strait of Hormuz](https://t.me/geopolitics_prime/68686), and systematic assaults on vessels in Gulf waters.

👍** **[**Boost us**](https://t.me/boost/geopolitics_prime)** | **[**Chat**](https://t.me/+tltgWD-GUqw0OTAy)** | ****@geopolitics_prime**
headline: **📈**** Gutted Gulf oil could roar back in months if Hormuz opens - Goldman Sachs

full_text:
🤥✡️ **Israel lobbyist & liar on tape: Trump’s WH gatekeeper Susie Wiles **

Donald Trump once famously promised to "drain the swamp" and cull lobbyists. 

Instead, he brought in veteran influence-peddling Republican Susie Wiles as his White House chief of staff in 2024.

🤝 Her muddy lobbying trail had long earned her a nickname that stuck - “Swamp creature” Wiles.

Most notorious was her [fixer role ](https://t.me/geopolitics_prime/56654)for genocidal Zionist Benjamin Netanyahu in his 2020 campaign. Wiles was one of Trump's top advisors brought in to help revamp Netanyahu’s messaging with high-energy, Trump-style rallies — Netanyahu got re-elected.

Speculation has also long swirled that she was a key asset in Israeli influence operations, from the 2018 Jerusalem Embassy move to "facilitating communications" between Trump and Netanyahu during aggression against Iran.

💣 In the lead-up to the 2025 US-Israeli strikes on Iran, Wiles reportedly oversaw how [intelligence was filtered ](https://t.me/geopolitics_prime/56654)to Trump. She ostensibly helped “isolate” Trump alongside CIA Director John Ratcliffe while feeding hardline Mossad narratives about the "Iranian threat."

Between 2017 and 2024, Wiles represented dozens of corporate clients for big money, including tobacco giants like Swisher. 

According to a November 2025 filing, she served [as co-chair ](https://t.me/geopolitics_prime/49977)of lobbying firm Mercury Public Affairs while it was on Doha’s payroll. 
Watchdog groups later flagged concerns that ex-clients maintained access to the very offices she now oversees.

**Rare media meltdown**

In 2025, media-shy Wiles sat for a Vanity Fair tell-all that [backfired ](https://t.me/geopolitics_prime/61246)spectacularly.

🗣 She called Trump’s personality "that of an alcoholic" and labeled Elon Musk an "avowed ketamine user" and an "odd, odd duck."

Her attempt to dismiss the most damaging Musk-related quote — “That’s ridiculous… I wouldn’t have said it” — flopped when an audio recording exposed her lie.
